qudex:source.displayLabel: Audio extract from Interview with Mr. Keble
qudex:source.label: 2000_Int001_clip1

qudex:source.displayLabel: Web Resource: Abstract of the Edwardians book
qudex:source.label: http://books.google.co.uk/books/about/The_Edwardians.html?id=pIYOAAAAQAAJ&redir_esc=y

qudex:document.displayLabel: Audio extract from Interview with Mr. Keble
Web Resource: Abstract of the Edwardians book

qudex:memo.label: Historical note
qudex:memo.qudex:memoText: Movement of rural populations to urban centres led to the decline of many small-scale village tradesmen. However larger villages could still support business such as wheelwrights and blacksmiths that were central to the local economy.

qudex:memo.label: Image description
qudex:memo.qudex:memoText: Three generations of a family pose for a photograph in front of the family home and business c.1904. Behind them are various works in progress for these wheelwrights including an urban district council wheelbarrow.

qudex:memo.label: Description of audio
qudex:memo.qudex:memoText: This is a three and a half minute audio extract taken from part of the audio-taped interview with Mr. Keeble.

qudex:memo.label: Historical note
qudex:memo.qudex:memoText: The fragility of the rural economy increased after industrialisation as improvements in transportation meant more produce was imported from abroad rather than being produced internally. One response can be seen in this retail outlet which acts not only as a local post office but also draper, tea and tobacco merchant and general grocery shop.

qudex:memo.label: Image description
qudex:memo.qudex:memoText: Informal photograph taken outside Bicknacre post office

qudex:category.label: Car hire business, agent for grocer and brewery
Senior messenger for National Economic Development Office
Secretary Agent of Derby Labour Party
Helped husband in printing business (no payment)
